resources.string: Note: Some applications use Split APKs. If direct installation fails, please use the Play Store.
Location permission is required to view GPS and positioning data.
- The app detects actual sensor resolution (before pixel binning) using the sensor database and Camera2 API.
- Camera permission helps detect the sensor model and get more accurate resolution data.
No cameras detected or permission denied
There will be inconsistency between sensor resolution here and actual resolution provided by manufacturers due to Android Camera2 API limitation. Check the manufacturer for more details.
To provide accurate device information, this app needs the following permissions:
Camera: To detect sensor model and physical resolution.
Location: To get detailed Wi-Fi information.
Bluetooth: To detect Bluetooth version and hardware.
Phone: To detect cellular network details and SIM info.
Storage: To display information about storage capacity and usage.
Notifications: To show device status in the notification bar.
Note: Power button may not be detectable on all devices.
Cover the sensor to see the value drop.
Sensor not available on this device.
Hold the device to your ear. Do you hear a 440Hz tone?
Do you hear a loud 440Hz tone from the bottom speakers?
Did all colors render correctly without dead pixels?
Type to search across all device specs
Show live stats in notification center
Please plug in and unplug wired headphones to verify detection.
Liên Minh Huyền Thoại Tốc Chiến
Uses your wallpaper colors (Material You) on Android 12+
One accent color applied across the entire app
Increases text and border contrast for better readability
To install applications from the App Hub, the app needs permission to install from unknown sources.
Place your finger on the sensor
Bluetooth permission is required to display device information.
Cellular information requires Phone permission or hardware support.
Generate a professional PDF of your device specs
Not connected to any Wi-Fi network.
Bluetooth is disabled. Please enable it.
Track position and lock best satellite in real-time
Cellular information requires Phone permission or hardware support.
Floating shortcut button with quick actions
Enables the AssistiveTouch floating button overlay for quick access to system actions like Home, Back, Screenshot, and more.
AssistiveTouch needs Accessibility Service permission to perform system actions like Home, Back, and Screenshot.\n\nPlease enable \"Relab Control Center\" in Accessibility settings.
AssistiveTouch needs permission to display over other apps to show the floating button.
Displays critical system-level security parameters including root status, SELinux enforcement, storage encryption, bootloader lock status, and verity configurations.
Queries secure system properties, Android Keystore flags, kernel status nodes, and bootloader lock state APIs provided by the hardware abstraction layer.
Ensures the integrity of the operating system. Unlocked bootloaders or active root access can make the device vulnerable to security threats and restrict payment applications.
Detailed physical and optical characteristics of the camera hardware, such as image resolution, sensor size, pixel size, ISO range, shutter speeds, and crop factors.
Queries camera hardware capabilities using the Android Camera2 API to read physical sensor dimensions, focus ranges, and supported exposure settings.
Helps photography enthusiasts understand the optical performance, light sensitivity, and capture capabilities of the lens and sensor combination.
Displays details and status information about %1$s.
Queries the Android operating system APIs, hardware properties, and system files for %1$s details.
Helps monitor %1$s specifications, verify configurations, and diagnose hardware health.
Identifies the unique marketing name, hardware brand, and manufacturer of your device.
Queries standard build properties configured by the device manufacturer.
Helps check device authenticity, look up manuals, or find compatible accessories.
The current version of the Android operating system running on your phone.
Retrieves the version release string from the Android system build properties.
Determines application compatibility and defines access to system features and security APIs.
The custom user interface layer or skin configured by your phone manufacturer.
Detects specific manufacturer build properties to determine custom UI versions.
Determines system UI themes, multitasking layouts, and pre-installed features.
The System-on-Chip containing CPU cores, graphics GPU, and memory controllers.
Reads internal kernel logs and hardware properties to identify the chipset model.
The primary driver of computing speed, graphics performance, and battery efficiency.
Displays battery state of charge, capacity, health, and current power source.
Monitors voltage, temperature, and current from the battery controller using BatteryManager.
Crucial to monitor battery capacity degradation, charging health, and optimize power savings.
Marketing name, manufacturer, brand, motherboard, and hardware identification parameters of the device.
Reads system build configuration values, manufacturer registries, and hardware identifier files.
Essential for verifying device authenticity, matching accessories, and identifying specific hardware revisions.
Android operating system properties, Linux kernel version, system build identifiers, and Google Play services availability.
Queries the Linux system kernel info (/proc/version), build descriptors, and GMS package registries.
Determines app compatibility, software support lifecycle, runtime library versions, and overall system security features.
Custom user interface shell, software overlay, or launcher skin configured by the device manufacturer.
Detects custom OS framework build variables (e.g., One UI, HyperOS, ColorOS) from system properties.
Controls custom multitasking options, user interface styling, navigation gestures, and custom vendor features.
Processor architecture specifications, CPU core configurations, cluster frequencies, fabrication technology, and instruction sets.
Parses processor register files (/proc/cpuinfo), reads core speed nodes, and queries supported ABI architectures.
Directly determines processing speeds, multitasking efficiency, thermal outputs, and app execution speed.
Graphics processing hardware, GPU clock speeds, driver versions, and supported rendering capabilities like Vulkan and OpenGL.
Queries OpenGL rendering context, Vulkan driver properties, and GPU load statistics from graphics controllers.
Controls 3D rendering speeds, gaming framerates, UI transitions, and video playback acceleration.
Detailed display panel parameters, resolution grids, refresh frequencies, color depth bits, and density configuration specs.
Queries WindowManager, active display frameworks, and graphics driver capabilities.
Influences screen sharpness, text readability, visual animation smoothness, color range accuracy, and battery consumption.
Volatile memory (RAM) capacity, zRAM swap space, internal storage partitions, block sizes, and filesystem formats (EXT4/F2FS).
Queries system memory info (/proc/meminfo), disk mounts, and block allocation tables.
Determines how many apps can stay active in the background, file transfer speeds, and storage longevity.
Battery charge levels, chemical health metrics, current input voltages, charging wattage, temperatures, and power sources.
Monitors BatteryManager API and registers broadcast receivers for system power supply nodes.
Crucial for preventing cell damage, checking charging efficiency, and monitoring lithium battery degradation over time.
Wireless network card properties, local frequencies, router connection statistics, IP addressing, and DNS resolvers.
Queries Android WifiManager and ConnectivityManager APIs to scan network states and DHCP info.
Determines local signal quality, local network throughput, IP configurations, and router connection speeds.
Bluetooth adapter status, protocol versions, paired hardware lists, and active profile connections.
Queries the local BluetoothAdapter system service to list capabilities, MAC addresses, and paired devices.
Manages short-range connections to wireless audio, wearables, keyboards, and file transfer services.
Cellular modem parameters, SIM card registers, carrier networks, cell towers, roaming states, and mobile network bands.
Queries the TelephonyManager API and reads system SIM slot information.
Determines mobile voice and data reception strength, APN profiles, carrier compatibility, and roaming registers.
Digital Rights Management licenses (Widevine levels) and hardware-accelerated media decoders and encoders.
Queries the MediaDrm system service and lists MediaCodec codec capabilities registered in the OS.
Determines if your device supports high-definition streaming on Netflix/YouTube, and improves video playing efficiency.
List of physical micro-sensors (accelerometer, gyroscope, light, pressure) and their polling frequencies.
Queries Android SensorManager to retrieve all available hardware sensor devices.
Enables automatic screen rotation, activity tracking, compass orientation, and gaming motion controls.
USB controller configs, OTG host features, and currently connected external USB peripherals.
Interfaces with UsbManager service to query connected USB devices and class interfaces.
Enables connecting external flash drives, mouse, keyboards, or accessories directly to the device.
Real-time thermal sensor zone temperatures on the motherboard and major microchips.
Reads temperature values from system thermal zone files in /sys/class/thermal.
Prevents hardware damage by triggering cooling throttles when CPU/GPU gets too hot.
System security patches, root access flags, SELinux enforcement, storage encryption keys, and Play Integrity trust levels.
Checks system binaries, reads SELinux context status, and performs Google Play Integrity checks.
Ensures operating system integrity, data safety, and prevents unauthorized access to user data.
Optical properties of camera lenses, aperture settings, sensor pixel sizes, video qualities, and shooting modes.
Queries Camera2 API for physical lens parameters and supported modes.
Determines photo detail, low-light image quality, field of view, and video capabilities.
The number of independent physical processing units (cores) within the Central Processing Unit (CPU).
Reads active CPU core configurations from system kernel interface nodes (/sys/devices/system/cpu).
More CPU cores allow the device to execute multiple applications and tasks concurrently, leading to better multi-threaded performance and multitasking.
The Graphics Processing Unit, a dedicated hardware processor designed for rendering 3D graphics, user interface animations, and visual effects.
Queries the active system OpenGL ES context and Vulkan driver properties to identify the exact GPU model.
A faster GPU handles gaming, UI animations, video rendering, and display refreshes smoother and more efficiently.
The real-time internal temperature of the lithium battery cell, measured in Celsius or Fahrenheit.
Monitors hardware thermistor sensors connected to the battery charging chip via the BatteryManager API.
Keeping battery temperature within safe limits prevents thermal runaway, charging issues, and long-term chemical capacity degradation.
The instantaneous power consumption or charging rate of the battery, measured in Watts (Voltage multiplied by Current).
Reads real-time battery voltage and current draw from the micro-fuel gauge chip via BatteryManager.
Helps track active charging speeds, diagnostic power adapters, cable compatibility, and background power drain.
The current power source feeding energy to the device, such as AC charger, USB port, or wireless pad.
Retrieves the power source status broadcasted by the Android battery service framework.
Helps diagnose charging issues and explains why charging might be slow on standard USB ports compared to fast wall chargers.
Indicates whether the battery is currently charging wirelessly via electromagnetic induction.
Detects if the wireless induction coil system is active and receiving power from a compatible transmitter pad.
Wireless charging is convenient but slower and generates more heat than traditional wired charging methods.
The total number of distinct pixels across the screen\'s width and height, representing detail capability.
Queries the active WindowManager service for the physical pixel layout of the main display screen.
Higher resolutions render text, images, and games with greater sharpness and clarity, though they consume slightly more power.
The bit depth used to represent colors on the screen, typically 8-bit (16.7 million colors) or 10-bit (1.07 billion colors).
Reads the graphics frame buffer configurations and active display controller color profiles.
Higher color depth reduces color banding, providing smoother gradients and more realistic media reproduction.
The number of times the screen redraws its image per second, measured in Hertz (Hz).
Queries the active Display system window configuration for current refresh rate frequencies.
Higher refresh rates (e.g. 90Hz, 120Hz) make animations, list scrolling, and gaming look incredibly fluid and smooth.
The diagonal measurement of the physical display panel, measured in inches from corner to corner.
Calculates physical screen size using the screen\'s resolution and pixels-per-inch density metrics.
Determines the screen real estate, aspect ratio, hand usability, and display scaling configurations.
The color space gamut support (like DCI-P3 or sRGB) indicating the range of colors the display panel can reproduce.
Queries the display driver properties for wide color gamut capability flags.
A wider color gamut enables more vivid, lifelike colors, which is essential for accurate photo editing and HDR content.
The core Bluetooth standard specification version supported by the device\'s wireless chipset.
Queries the system Bluetooth manager service for hardware controller capabilities.
Newer Bluetooth versions (like 5.x) offer longer range, faster data transfers, and better battery efficiency for wireless audio.
The number and list of wireless accessories that have completed a secure pairing handshake with this device.
Queries the local Bluetooth adapter database for saved cryptographic pairing keys.
Allows quick, secure connections to your audio equipment, smartwatches, and other accessories.
The list of Bluetooth devices that are currently active and transmitting or receiving data.
Queries active Bluetooth profiles (like A2DP audio or BLE) for established connections.
Shows which specific speaker, headphone, or smartwatch is actively communicating with your phone.
The wireless network interface card, showing connection details like frequency, RSSI, and protocol standard.
Queries the system WifiManager service for connection details and receiver signal strength.
Helps diagnose signal strength issues, channel interference, and wireless network performance.
The cellular modem status, including signal reception strength, network generation (4G/5G), and registration status.
Reads signal information and registration states from the TelephonyManager service.
Essential for tracking mobile voice and data coverage, connection reliability, and cellular provider registration.
The total count of user-installed and system applications loaded on the device.
Queries the Android Package Manager to retrieve the list of all active packages.
Helps audit device storage footprint, background app load, and identify potential bloatware or malware.
The release date of the Google Android security patch level currently installed on the firmware.
Reads the security patch release version string from system build properties.
Indicates the level of protection against known vulnerabilities, exploits, and security flaws.
The size of the lens opening (f-number) which controls how much light reaches the camera sensor.
Queries camera lens characteristics using the Android Camera2 API.
Wider apertures (lower f-number like f/1.8) let in more light for night photography and create a beautiful shallow depth of field (bokeh).
The focal length of the camera lens, determining the angle of view and magnification.
Queries physical lens specifications from the Camera2 characteristics database.
Differentiates wide-angle lenses (short focal length) from telephoto lenses (long focal length) for zoom capability.
The physical size of individual pixels on the image sensor, measured in micrometers (microns).
Calculates pixel size using the camera\'s physical sensor dimensions and resolution grid.
Larger pixels capture more photons, resulting in significantly better low-light performance and less image noise.
The maximum image resolution of the camera sensor, measured in Megapixels (MP).
Reads the maximum image capture size configurations supported by the camera lens.
Higher resolutions capture finer details, allowing you to crop and enlarge photos without losing clarity.
The maximum video recording resolution and framerate capability of the camera sensor.
Queries supported video profiles and stream configurations from the Camera2 API.
Determines capability to capture ultra-high-definition video (like 4K or 8K) and high frame rates for slow motion.
The hardware board name and platform chipset identifier code name configured by the chip designer.
Queries system boot properties and kernel board variables (ro.board.platform).
Helps developers identify the exact baseboard chipset for firmware updates and low-level customization.
Detailed Android operating system configurations, including runtime, uptime, and library variables.
Reads core system properties, Linux kernel version info, and runtime libraries.
Defines application compatibility, system lifecycle, runtime features, and basic OS configurations.
The compilation descriptors, signature tags, and specific build version identifier of the ROM.
Queries system properties configured by the build compiler (ro.build.display.id).
Crucial for identifying the exact firmware version, tracking updates, and debugging software bugs.
Overall display metric settings, density configurations, and layout scale factors.
Queries active window screen dimensions and density properties from resources config.
Ensures the operating system scales UI buttons, text, and graphics properly across different physical display sizes.
Unique identifiers used to verify the specific device build and hardware authenticity.
Queries standard build serial codes and unique fingerprint strings defined in system build parameters.
Used by system update servers to deliver correct OTA updates and verify Google service certification.
Various system statistics, including bootloader status, JVM runtime type, and uptime counter.
Queries system runtime properties and checks system clock boot parameters.
Provides insights into the overall runtime stability, boot state, and system configurations.
The storage filesystem structures, partition formats, and block write configurations of the storage chip.
Reads storage mount registers and filesystems (like F2FS or EXT4) listed in the kernel mount tables.
Different filesystems affect reading and writing speeds, application launching times, and long-term storage endurance.
The active network connectivity channels routing data traffic on the device.
Monitors the system network connectivity service for active routing interfaces.
Shows how the system routes traffic, managing handoffs between mobile data and Wi-Fi to keep connections active.
Advanced Wi-Fi connection configurations, local IP routing, and wireless chip parameters.
Queries current Wi-Fi connection descriptors and network configuration files.
Useful for debugging network connection drops, verifying signal strength, and checking IP setup.
Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol lease settings, DNS servers, and network gateway addresses.
Reads active DHCP server allocations and DNS configurations from WifiManager.
Diagnoses connection issues caused by IP conflicts, invalid routing gateways, or slow DNS resolvers.
SIM card status, including country codes, carrier locks, and subscription states.
Queries subscription info and slot status using TelephonyManager.
Identifies if the SIM is registered correctly and helps troubleshoot mobile reception or activation issues.
Mobile network operator name, country code, and network registration status.
Reads network operator details from the active cell towers registered to the SIM.
Verifies which carrier network you are connected to and whether you are currently roaming.
The security status of physical biometric unlock systems (fingerprint and facial recognition).
Queries biometric hardware presence and enrollment status from the BiometricManager service.
Confirms that biometric locks are properly set up to protect your private data and secure banking apps.
Google Play Integrity status, verifying if the device runs a trusted, certified Android build.
Performs system checkups to verify if the OS integrity matches certified Google build records.
Required by security-sensitive apps (like banking, streaming, and mobile payments) to protect against fraud.
Shows the storage encryption state, securing files against physical data extraction.
Queries the system storage manager to check if File-Based Encryption (FBE) is active.
Ensures that your personal data, passwords, and files remain completely private and secure even if the device is lost.
The security status of active network connections, checking for VPN usage or customized secure DNS.
Inspects active network interfaces and queries system DNS configuration rules.
Helps ensure that your network traffic is encrypted and protected against snooping on public networks.
An audit of apps granted highly sensitive system permissions, like Camera, Location, and Microphone.
Queries the system Package Manager to search for applications with active sensitive permissions.
Allows you to easily review which applications have access to your private sensors, helping prevent privacy leaks.
App security monitoring, tracking sideloaded apps and active device administrator applications.
Scans package installers and lists applications registered as device administrators.
Allows you to review potential security threats from apps that bypass standard App stores or hold elevated system control.
The status of Developer Options, USB Debugging (ADB), and bootloader lock settings.
Reads system settings flags to verify developer mode configurations.
Enabling USB debugging allows command-line computer access to the device, which should be disabled for safety when not in use.
Battery design specs, charge cycle count, and calculated capacity state.
Reads the hardware fuel gauge\'s historical charge cycles and original capacity specs.
Determines the battery health degradation by comparing current capacity to original specifications.
Volatile Random Access Memory (RAM) specs and active zRAM swap storage utilization.
Reads RAM variables from kernel memory allocation stats (/proc/meminfo).
Determines multitasking capability, indicating how many apps can stay active in the background without reloading.
The hardware flash storage layout, total capacity, and active partition free space.
Queries file system disk space properties for the user storage directory.
Indicates available space for photos, apps, and documents. Running out of space slows down the operating system.
Historical records of battery usage patterns, charge levels, and voltage profiles over time.
Collects battery status logs from system stats database registries.
Helps identify which applications or hardware features are causing excessive battery drain.
Detailed CPU architecture configuration, ABI support, and clock frequency bounds.
Reads processor specifications and frequency settings from kernel parameters.
Shows the capabilities of the CPU chip, indicating which software instructions and architectures are natively supported.
Supported instruction sets and CPU features (like NEON, AES encryption, SHA).
Queries CPU feature register flags from the system hardware identification lists.
Enables faster video rendering, gaming physics, and cryptographic calculations through hardware acceleration.
The fabrication technology node (measured in nanometers) used to manufacture the chipset.
Queries board database specifications and chip hardware identifiers.
Smaller fabrication nodes (like 4nm or 3nm) mean smaller transistors, which are significantly more energy-efficient and run cooler.
Advanced Bluetooth profiles, supported services, and hardware adapter addresses.
Queries the local Bluetooth controller services for active configuration profiles.
Determines which audio codecs, wireless controllers, and wearable data exchange features are supported.
Motherboard thermal sensors monitoring real-time temperatures of critical components.
Reads active sensor values from system thermal zone registries (/sys/class/thermal).
Helps prevent hardware damage by checking if the system is throttling performance to cool down overheating chips.
Google Play system update levels and dynamic security component versions.
Queries package manager updates for critical security framework libraries.
Enables critical security fixes to be installed directly from Google without requiring a full phone software update.
Digital Rights Management configurations, including Widevine certification levels.
Queries the MediaDrm API for Widevine DRM keys and security levels.
Determines media streaming capabilities. Widevine L1 is required to play high-definition (HD) media in apps like Netflix.
The list of hardware and software-based media encoders and decoders registered in the OS.
Queries the system MediaCodecList database for supported audio and video formats.
Hardware codecs decode videos (like H.264 or AV1) with extremely low battery consumption, saving power during playback.
USB connection configurations, active controller settings, and connected peripherals.
Queries the system USB manager service for connected hardware interfaces.
Enables using USB accessories (OTG) such as flash drives, mouse, keyboards, or MIDI tools.
Complete inventory of physical sensors (gyroscope, accelerometer, barometer, light) available.
Queries the Android SensorManager to retrieve descriptions of all registered sensors.
Allows applications to read movement, tilt, brightness, and atmospheric pressure for UI, gaming, and navigation.
The camera software capabilities (HDR, Night Mode, Portrait, Burst) supported.
Queries camera capabilities and shooting modes from the Camera2 characteristics.
Determines visual capture features, allowing the user to take advanced photographs under various lighting conditions.
